title: 16S rRNA Library Preparation Protocol
author: Kara Tinker
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.jz4cp8w

steps.title: Dilute Sample DNA
steps.instruction: Dilute sample DNA to ~2-5ng/μl. If the sample is <2ng/μl, use sample as is.

steps.title: Prepare First PCR Reaction Mix
steps.instruction: Sample PCR should be done in duplicate, one serving as the base sample and another as a technical replicate. Prepare reaction mix for #of reactions +1 to prevent shortage of mix. Reagents for one 10μl reaction are as follows: H2O – 4.7 μl 5X Q5 Hot Start Buffer - 2.0 μl 10μM Fprimer (505F)- 0.5 μl 10μM Rprimer (806R)- 0.5 μl 10mM dNTPs- 0.2 μlQ5 Hot Start DNA Polymerase- 0.1 μl Distribute 8 μl of reaction mix to reaction tubes and then add 2 μl of diluted DNA (total 10μl).(Note: Add reagents as listed. 5X buffer and primers may be thawed at room temperature but dNTPs should be thawed on top of ice and polymerase should be kept on ice at all times.)

substeps.instruction: Sample PCR should be done in duplicate, one serving as the base sample and another as a technical replicate. Prepare reaction mix for #of reactions +1 to prevent shortage of mix. Reagents for one 10μl reaction are as follows: H2O –

μl Distribute 8 μl of reaction mix to reaction tubes and then add 2 μl of diluted DNA (total 10μl).(Note: Add reagents as listed. 5X buffer and primers may be thawed at room temperature but dNTPs should be thawed on top of ice and polymerase should be kept on ice at all times.)

steps.title: Prepare Second PCR Reaction Mix
steps.instruction: Prepare 2 nd reaction mix while the first reaction is running. Prepare reaction mix for # of reactions +1. Reagents for one 30ml reaction are as follows: H2O- 11.1 μl 5X Q5 Hot Start Buffer- 6.0 μl 10μM 806_BCR primer- 1.5 μl 10mM dNTPs- 0.6 μl Q5 Hot Start DNA Polymerase – 0.3 μl Remove reactions from thermocycler once the first reaction is completed. Keep reactions on cold block. Distribute 21μl of 2 nd reaction mix to each new reaction tube. Add 9μl of reaction 1 product to the appropriate 2 nd reaction tube. Pipette up and down to mix.

steps.title: Purify PCR Reactions
steps.instruction: Purify reactions using Omega Bio-Tek’s E.Z.N.A. Cycle-Pure Kit according to manufacturer’s protocol.

steps.title: Quantify and Store Samples
steps.instruction: Measure sample concentration and A260/A280 via nanodrop, record values. Store at -20°C.

steps.title: Verify PCR Product by Gel
steps.instruction: Verify PCR product by gel electrophoresis.

steps.title: Create Subpools of Amplicons
steps.instruction: Pool together an equal amount of amplicons (generally 50ng, but adjust if necessary) from each sample into subpools composed of 15-30 unique samples.

steps.title: Verify Subpool Products by Gel
steps.instruction: Verify subpool products by gel electrophoresis.

steps.title: Combine Subpools for Sequencing
steps.instruction: Combine an equal amount of amplicons from each subpool and submit the pooled library for sequencing.
